texto: So all I’m saying is take that tiara back out, place it on your head, and prance around like a goddess. Paint your face, paint those pictures, paint the entire world if that’s what youwant. Leave your colorful mark in this school, and together, we’ll wear our hearts on our sleeves and make the area explode with spouts of laughter.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ayla Starr: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “So all I’m saying is take that tiara back out, place it on your head, and prance around like a go…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, letter, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de love e romantic.

frase_original: So all I’m saying is take that tiara back out, place it on your head, and prance around like a goddess. Paint your face, paint those pictures, paint the entire world if that’s what youwant. Leave your colorful mark in this school, and together, we’ll wear our hearts on our sleeves and make the area explode with spouts of laughter.
